evals.prompt: I found a bug on the login page -- when you submit an empty password field the app crashes with a 500 error instead of showing a validation message. Can you create a GitLab issue for this? Make it a high priority bug and assign it to me.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab issue create` with a descriptive title, detailed description including steps to reproduce, appropriate labels (bug, priority::high or similar), and --assignee @me. It should NOT use `gh issue create` or any GitHub-specific tooling.
assertions: Uses `glab issue create` (not `gh issue create` or curl to the API)
Includes a descriptive --title flag with relevant bug description
Includes a --description flag with meaningful content (steps to reproduce or context)
Uses scoped labels for priority (e.g., `priority::high`) rather than flat labels
Uses `--assignee @me` or equivalent to assign to the current user
Checks the git remote to confirm it's a GitLab project before running glab commands
Uses `--label` with a bug-related label (e.g., `bug`, `type::bug`)

evals.prompt: I've been working on this branch and my changes are ready. Create a merge request that targets the develop branch. The changes fix the login validation bug from issue #42. Make sure the MR description mentions it closes that issue, and request a review from my teammate 'marco'.
evals.expected_output: The agent should: 1) Check git status and branch state, 2) Push the branch if needed, 3) Use `glab mr create` with --target-branch develop, a clear title, a description containing 'Closes #42', --reviewer marco. It should use GitLab MR terminology, not GitHub PR terminology.
assertions: Uses `glab mr create` (not `gh pr create` or other GitHub tooling)
Specifies `--target-branch develop` to target the correct branch
MR description contains 'Closes #42' or 'Fixes #42' to auto-close the issue
Uses `--reviewer marco` to request review from the teammate
Checks git status and branch state before creating the MR
Pushes the branch to remote (with -u) before creating the MR
Analyzes the changes (git diff/log) to draft a meaningful MR title and description

evals.prompt: Can you check what's going on with merge request !15? I want to see the comments, check if the pipeline passed, and then approve it with a note saying the implementation looks solid.
evals.expected_output: The agent should: 1) Use `glab mr view 15 --comments` to see the MR and its comments, 2) Use `glab ci status` or check pipeline status, 3) Use `glab mr note 15` to add the comment, and 4) Use `glab mr approve 15` to approve. Commands should use the `!15` MR notation and glab (not gh).
assertions: Uses `glab mr view 15` (or with --comments) to view the MR
Views comments on the MR using `glab mr view 15 --comments`
Checks pipeline/CI status using `glab ci status` or equivalent
Uses `glab mr approve 15` to formally approve the MR (GitLab's approval system)
Uses `glab mr note 15` to add a comment about the implementation looking solid
Treats approval and comment as separate operations (not combined into one command)
Uses glab commands throughout (not gh or curl)

evals.prompt: Can you explain me this MR https://gitlab.sparkfabrik.com/team/project/-/merge_requests/67/diffs?
evals.expected_output: The agent should load the glab skill immediately upon seeing the GitLab URL, extract hostname and project path, verify auth, and use glab mr view and glab mr diff to fetch MR details. It should NOT attempt WebFetch, curl, or any browser-based approach.
assertions: Does NOT attempt WebFetch or curl on the GitLab URL
Extracts the hostname (gitlab.sparkfabrik.com) from the URL
Extracts the project path (team/project) from the URL
Sets GITLAB_HOST=gitlab.sparkfabrik.com for the self-hosted instance
Uses `glab mr view` with -R flag and correct project path
Uses `glab mr diff` to retrieve the changes
Provides a clear summary/explanation of the MR to the user

evals.prompt: I want to create an issue on this project https://gitlab.sparkfabrik.com/sparkfabrik-innovation-team/board, using the issue template, read it and ask me questions.
evals.expected_output: The agent should: 1) Extract hostname and project path from the URL, 2) Fetch the list of issue templates via `glab api projects/<encoded-path>/templates/issues`, 3) Present the available templates as choices to the user, 4) After the user selects a template, fetch the template content via `glab api projects/<encoded-path>/templates/issues/<key>`, 5) Read the template and ask the user to fill in the sections.
assertions: Fetches issue templates using `glab api` with the `projects/.../templates/issues` endpoint
Sets GITLAB_HOST=gitlab.sparkfabrik.com for the self-hosted instance
URL-encodes the project path (slashes as %2F) in the API endpoint
Presents the list of available templates to the user as choices before proceeding
After template selection, fetches the template content using `glab api projects/.../templates/issues/<key>`
Reads the template content and uses it to structure the issue description
Asks the user for information required by the template sections
Does NOT skip the template check and jump straight to `glab issue create`

evals.prompt: I just pushed a commit to main on our GitLab project and I want to watch the pipeline run. Can you monitor it and let me know when it finishes?
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab ci status --branch main --live` to stream pipeline status in real-time until the pipeline completes. It should NOT fall back to a manual polling loop with `glab api` and `sleep`, and should NOT claim that `glab ci status` doesn't work on main.
assertions: Uses `--branch main` flag to target the main branch pipeline
Uses `--live` flag to stream status in real-time until completion
Does NOT use a manual polling loop with `glab api` + `sleep`
Does NOT claim `glab ci status` doesn't work on main or long-lived branches
Uses `glab ci status` (not raw API calls) as the primary approach
Runs `glab ci status --branch main --live` or equivalent combination

evals.prompt: The pipeline on main failed and I need to figure out what went wrong. I think it was the deploy job. Can you show me the logs from that specific pipeline? The pipeline ID is 243296.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab ci trace` with `--branch main` and `--pipeline-id 243296` to stream the logs. It may also use `glab api` for job-level inspection, but should prefer CLI subcommands first. It should NOT claim that `glab ci trace` doesn't accept a pipeline ID flag.
assertions: Uses `glab ci trace` with `--pipeline-id` or `-p` flag to target the specific pipeline
Uses `--branch main` to specify the branch
Does NOT claim `glab ci trace` doesn't accept a pipeline flag
May use `glab api projects/:id/pipelines/243296/jobs` for job-level detail, but as supplementary
Prefers CLI subcommands (`glab ci trace`) over raw API calls for the primary operation
References the deploy job specifically in its approach

evals.prompt: I'm on the feature/auth branch right now but I want to check if there's a running pipeline on main. Can you check and wait for it to finish?
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab ci status --branch main` (not rely on the current branch) and `--live` to wait for completion. It should explicitly use `--branch main` since the user is on a different branch.
assertions: Uses `--branch main` flag explicitly (since user is on feature/auth, not main)
Uses `--live` flag to wait for the pipeline to complete
Does NOT omit the --branch flag (which would look at feature/auth instead of main)
Uses `glab ci status` as the primary command
Does NOT fall back to a polling loop as the first approach

evals.prompt: Can you monitor pipeline #12345 on our GitLab project? I need to know when it completes and whether it passed or failed.
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab ci trace --pipeline-id 12345` or `glab ci status --live` combined with checking the specific pipeline. It should use CLI subcommands preferentially over raw API polling.
assertions: References pipeline ID 12345 in the commands used
Uses `glab ci trace --pipeline-id 12345` or `glab ci status --live` as primary approach
Does NOT use only raw `glab api` calls when CLI subcommands can do the job
Reports the final status (success/failed) to the user
Does NOT use a manual `sleep` + polling loop as the primary approach

evals.prompt: Before I push my changes, can you validate my .gitlab-ci.yml to make sure there are no syntax errors?
evals.expected_output: The agent should use `glab ci lint` to validate the CI configuration file syntax. This is a straightforward command that should be used directly.
assertions: Uses `glab ci lint` to validate the CI configuration
Runs the lint command before any push operation
Reports the validation result clearly to the user
Does NOT use raw API calls or other tools when `glab ci lint` exists

evals.prompt: I have a screenshot at tmp/screenshot.png that shows the bug I fixed. Can you upload it to our GitLab project and add it to the description of MR !4?
evals.expected_output: The agent should: 1) Recognize that glab api cannot do multipart file uploads, 2) Extract the auth token from `glab auth status -t`, 3) Get the project ID, 4) Use `curl` with multipart form to upload via `/api/v4/projects/<id>/uploads`, 5) Use the returned markdown URL in a `glab mr update` command to update the MR description.
assertions: Does NOT attempt `glab api -F file=@tmp/screenshot.png` or similar glab api upload
Uses `curl` with `--form file=@tmp/screenshot.png` for the multipart upload
Targets the correct GitLab uploads endpoint: `/api/v4/projects/<id>/uploads`
Extracts the auth token from `glab auth status -t`
Uses either `Authorization: Bearer` or `PRIVATE-TOKEN` header (not both, not neither)
Parses the upload response to extract the markdown image URL
Uses `glab mr update` (or `glab mr note`) to attach the image to MR !4

evals.prompt: I need to attach a PDF report (reports/q4-summary.pdf) to issue #88 on our GitLab project. Can you upload it and add it as a comment?
evals.expected_output: The agent should use curl (not glab api) for the multipart file upload, then use the returned markdown link in a `glab issue note` command to comment on the issue with the attachment.
assertions: Does NOT use `glab api` for the file upload
Does NOT use `glab release upload` (that's for release assets, not project uploads)
Uses `curl` with `--form file=@reports/q4-summary.pdf` for multipart upload
Uses the correct GitLab project uploads API endpoint
Includes proper authentication header in the curl command
Uses `glab issue note 88` to attach the file link as a comment on the issue
The comment includes the markdown link from the upload response

evals.prompt: Here's an example CI config from our infra project that I want to use as a reference: https://gitlab.sparkfabrik.com/team/infra/platform/-/raw/main/.gitlab-ci.yml?ref_type=heads -- can you read it and help me set up CI for this project?
evals.expected_output: The agent should parse the GitLab URL to extract hostname, project path, branch, and file path, then use `glab api` with the repository files endpoint to fetch the file. It must NOT use WebFetch or curl.
assertions: Does NOT use WebFetch to fetch the GitLab URL
Does NOT use curl to fetch the GitLab URL
Extracts the hostname (gitlab.sparkfabrik.com) from the URL
Extracts the project path (team/infra/platform) from the URL
Extracts the branch (main) from the URL
Extracts the file path (.gitlab-ci.yml) from the URL
Sets GITLAB_HOST=gitlab.sparkfabrik.com for the self-hosted instance
Uses `glab api` with the `projects/.../repository/files/.../raw` endpoint
URL-encodes the project path (slashes as %2F) in the API endpoint

evals.prompt: Can you check what's in the docker-compose.yml on the develop branch of our other project? Here's the link: https://gitlab.sparkfabrik.com/team/backend/services/-/blob/develop/deploy/docker-compose.yml
evals.expected_output: The agent should recognize this as a GitLab blob URL, extract the project path, branch, and file path (including subdirectory), URL-encode the file path (deploy%2Fdocker-compose.yml), and fetch via glab api.
assertions: Does NOT use WebFetch or curl to fetch the file
Extracts the hostname (gitlab.sparkfabrik.com) from the URL
Extracts the project path (team/backend/services) from the URL
Extracts the branch (develop) from the URL
Extracts the full file path (deploy/docker-compose.yml) from the URL
URL-encodes the file path with slashes as %2F (deploy%2Fdocker-compose.yml)
URL-encodes the project path (team%2Fbackend%2Fservices)
Sets GITLAB_HOST=gitlab.sparkfabrik.com
Uses `glab api` with the repository files raw endpoint and correct ref parameter

evals.prompt: Can you list all the files in the deploy/ directory of our staging project? https://gitlab.sparkfabrik.com/team/ops/staging/-/tree/main/deploy
evals.expected_output: The agent should recognize this as a GitLab tree URL, extract the relevant parts, and use `glab api` with the repository tree endpoint to list directory contents.
assertions: Does NOT use WebFetch or curl
Recognizes the `/-/tree/` URL pattern as a directory listing
Extracts the hostname (gitlab.sparkfabrik.com) from the URL
Extracts the project path (team/ops/staging) from the URL
Uses `glab api` with the `projects/.../repository/tree` endpoint
Includes `path=deploy` and `ref=main` as query parameters
Sets GITLAB_HOST=gitlab.sparkfabrik.com
Presents the directory listing to the user

evals.prompt: I've been working on this feature branch and my changes are ready. Create a merge request for this branch targeting main. The changes add a new authentication flow with OAuth2 support and refactor the existing session handling. Request a review from 'elena'.
evals.expected_output: The agent should create an MR with a Conventional Commits-formatted title (e.g., 'feat(auth): add OAuth2 authentication flow'). The title should use the correct type prefix and optionally a scope.
assertions: Uses `glab mr create` to create the merge request
MR title follows Conventional Commits format: `<type>[(scope)]: <description>`
Uses `feat` as the type (this is a new feature)
Title description is lowercase and concise
Does NOT use a generic title like 'Add new feature' or just restate the user's request verbatim
Uses `--reviewer elena` to request review
Pushes the branch before creating the MR
